carryover_case: short proof phrase retained inside the definition excerpt
why_it_is_tempting: It is directly adjacent, short, and may benefit understanding.
why_it_may_be_allowed_here: The owner says it is insignificantly short and harmless in this specific case.
why_it_does_not_change_the_underlying_on: Definition and proof remain different units even if packaged together.
when_it_stops_being_harmless: When the proof becomes longer, more independent, or leaf-polluting.

carryover_case: definition/proof retained inside the attribution excerpt
why_it_is_tempting: Attribution attaches to those exact contents.
why_it_may_be_allowed_here: The whole stretch is short and tightly related.
why_it_does_not_change_the_underlying_on: The attribution layer remains attribution, not a definition-proof merger in ontology.
when_it_stops_being_harmless: When proof text becomes long enough that context-fill should replace carrying it wholesale.

why_it_is_tempting: Knowing whose interpretation it is can be highly informative under the proof branch.
why_it_may_be_allowed_here: The owner explicitly says this can be especially important in proof excerpts.
why_it_does_not_change_the_underlying_on: Importance of relation does not erase distinct roles.
when_it_stops_being_harmless: When attribution becomes so long that it burdens proof study rather than supports it.

why_it_may_be_allowed_here: Only in tightly bounded short cases, not as a blanket rule.
why_it_does_not_change_the_underlying_on: Packaging convenience cannot redefine subject identity.
when_it_stops_being_harmless: As soon as it creates confusion, pollution, or hides distinct leaf-worthy units.
